---
title: "Confidential Asset (CA)"
description: "Complete guide to working with confidential assets on Aptos using the TypeScript SDK, including ZK-proofs, encryption, transfers, and key rotation"
sidebar:
  label: "Confidential Assets"
---

import { Aside } from '@astrojs/starlight/components';

The `@aptos-labs/confidential-assets` package provides a high-level TypeScript API for interacting with the Confidential Asset protocol on Aptos.

### Installation

```bash
npm install @aptos-labs/confidential-assets @aptos-labs/ts-sdk @aptos-labs/confidential-asset-wasm-bindings
```

### Initialization

Operations in CA require ZK-proof generation and discrete log solving, both powered by a unified WASM module.
Initialize it once at the top of your app:

```typescript
import { initializeWasm } from "@aptos-labs/confidential-assets";

await initializeWasm();
```

This initializes both the range proof system (for generating ZKPs) and the discrete log solver (for decrypting balances).

<Aside type="note">
  In Node.js, the WASM binary is loaded from `node_modules` automatically.
  In the browser, it is fetched from a CDN. You can pass a custom WASM source URL or `BufferSource` to `initializeWasm()`.
</Aside>

For native apps, you can generate Android and iOS bindings from the
[confidential-asset-wasm-bindings](https://github.com/aptos-labs/confidential-asset-wasm-bindings) repository.

***

Now create the `ConfidentialAsset` client:

```typescript
import { AptosConfig, Network } from "@aptos-labs/ts-sdk";
import { ConfidentialAsset } from "@aptos-labs/confidential-assets";

const config = new AptosConfig({ network: Network.TESTNET });
const confidentialAsset = new ConfidentialAsset({ config });
```

### Create Decryption Key (DK)

To interact with the confidential asset, create a [unique key pair](/build/smart-contracts/confidential-asset#confidential-store) first.

Generate new:

```typescript
import { TwistedEd25519PrivateKey } from "@aptos-labs/confidential-assets";

const dk = TwistedEd25519PrivateKey.generate();
```

Or import an existing one:

```typescript
const dk = new TwistedEd25519PrivateKey("0x...");
```

You can also derive it from a signature (for testing purposes, don't use in production):

```typescript
import { Account } from "@aptos-labs/ts-sdk";

const user = Account.generate();
const signature = user.sign(TwistedEd25519PrivateKey.decryptionKeyDerivationMessage);
const dk = TwistedEd25519PrivateKey.fromSignature(signature);
```

### Register

Before using confidential balances, [register](/build/smart-contracts/confidential-asset#register) an encryption key for the asset type:

```typescript
const registerTxResponse = await confidentialAsset.registerBalance({
  signer: user,
  tokenAddress: TOKEN_ADDRESS,
  decryptionKey: dk,
});
```

Check if a user has already registered for a specific asset type:

```typescript
const isRegistered = await confidentialAsset.hasUserRegistered({
  accountAddress: user.accountAddress,
  tokenAddress: TOKEN_ADDRESS,
});
```

### Deposit

[Deposit](/build/smart-contracts/confidential-asset#deposit) tokens from a non-confidential FA balance into a confidential balance:

```typescript
const depositTxResponse = await confidentialAsset.deposit({
  signer: user,
  tokenAddress: TOKEN_ADDRESS,
  amount: 100n,
});
```

### Get User's Balance

Check the user's balance after the deposit. The `getBalance` method returns the decrypted pending and available balances:

```typescript
const balance = await confidentialAsset.getBalance({
  accountAddress: user.accountAddress,
  tokenAddress: TOKEN_ADDRESS,
  decryptionKey: dk,
});

console.log("Available:", balance.availableBalance()); // bigint
console.log("Pending:", balance.pendingBalance());     // bigint
```

<Aside type="note">
  `availableBalance()` returns the balance that can be spent (via transfer or withdrawal).
  `pendingBalance()` returns incoming funds that need to be rolled over first.
</Aside>

### Rollover

After depositing, the funds are in the `pending` balance. A user cannot spend `pending` balance directly —
it must be [rolled over](/build/smart-contracts/confidential-asset#rollover-pending-balance) to the `available` balance.

<Aside type="caution">
  The available balance must be [normalized](/build/smart-contracts/confidential-asset#normalize) before a rollover.
  The SDK handles this automatically if you provide a `senderDecryptionKey`.
</Aside>

```typescript
const rolloverTxResponses = await confidentialAsset.rolloverPendingBalance({
  signer: user,
  tokenAddress: TOKEN_ADDRESS,
  senderDecryptionKey: dk, // Required if balance might not be normalized
});
```

This may return multiple transaction responses (a normalization transaction followed by a rollover transaction)
if the available balance was not already normalized.

***

### Normalization

Usually you don't need to call [normalization](/build/smart-contracts/confidential-asset#normalize) explicitly —
`rolloverPendingBalance` handles it automatically when given a `senderDecryptionKey`.

If you want to normalize manually:

<Aside type="caution">
  Check if the balance is already normalized before calling this — normalizing an already-normalized balance will fail.
</Aside>

```typescript
const isNormalized = await confidentialAsset.isBalanceNormalized({
  accountAddress: user.accountAddress,
  tokenAddress: TOKEN_ADDRESS,
});

if (!isNormalized) {
  const normalizeTxResponse = await confidentialAsset.normalizeBalance({
    signer: user,
    senderDecryptionKey: dk,
    tokenAddress: TOKEN_ADDRESS,
  });
}
```

### Withdraw

[Withdraw](/build/smart-contracts/confidential-asset#withdraw) assets from a confidential balance back to a non-confidential FA balance:

```typescript
const withdrawTxResponse = await confidentialAsset.withdraw({
  signer: user,
  senderDecryptionKey: dk,
  tokenAddress: TOKEN_ADDRESS,
  amount: 50n,
  recipient: recipientAddress, // Optional — defaults to signer's address
});
```

If the available balance is insufficient but the total (available + pending) is enough,
use `withdrawWithTotalBalance` which automatically rolls over the pending balance first:

```typescript
const withdrawTxResponses = await confidentialAsset.withdrawWithTotalBalance({
  signer: user,
  senderDecryptionKey: dk,
  tokenAddress: TOKEN_ADDRESS,
  amount: 50n,
});
```

### Transfer

For a [confidential transfer](/build/smart-contracts/confidential-asset#confidential-transfer), you need the recipient's account address.
The SDK automatically fetches the recipient's on-chain encryption key:

```typescript
const transferTxResponse = await confidentialAsset.transfer({
  signer: user,
  recipient: recipientAddress,
  tokenAddress: TOKEN_ADDRESS,
  amount: 50n,
  senderDecryptionKey: dk,
  additionalAuditorEncryptionKeys: [], // Optional voluntary auditors
});
```

<Aside type="note">
  If an effective auditor (global or asset-specific) is configured, the SDK automatically
  includes the auditor's ciphertext in the transfer. You do not need to add it manually.
</Aside>

Like with withdrawals, you can use `transferWithTotalBalance` to automatically roll over the pending balance
if the available balance is insufficient:

```typescript
const transferTxResponses = await confidentialAsset.transferWithTotalBalance({
  signer: user,
  recipient: recipientAddress,
  tokenAddress: TOKEN_ADDRESS,
  amount: 50n,
  senderDecryptionKey: dk,
});
```

You can also look up a user's encryption key directly:

```typescript
const recipientEK = await confidentialAsset.getEncryptionKey({
  accountAddress: recipientAddress,
  tokenAddress: TOKEN_ADDRESS,
});
```

### Key Rotation

To [rotate](/build/smart-contracts/confidential-asset#rotate-encryption-key) the encryption key, provide the current and new decryption keys.
The SDK handles pausing incoming transfers, rolling over pending balance, rotating the key, and resuming transfers:

```typescript
const newDk = TwistedEd25519PrivateKey.generate();

const rotationTxResponses = await confidentialAsset.rotateEncryptionKey({
  signer: user,
  senderDecryptionKey: dk,
  newSenderDecryptionKey: newDk,
  tokenAddress: TOKEN_ADDRESS,
});

// Save the new decryption key — the old one is no longer valid
console.log("New DK:", newDk.toString());
```

<Aside type="caution">
  After key rotation, you must use the new decryption key for all subsequent operations.
  The old key will no longer be able to decrypt the balance.
</Aside>

### Pause/Unpause Incoming Transfers

Check if a user's incoming transfers are paused:

```typescript
const isPaused = await confidentialAsset.isIncomingTransfersPaused({
  accountAddress: user.accountAddress,
  tokenAddress: TOKEN_ADDRESS,
});
```

### Auditors

Query auditor configuration for an asset type:

```typescript
// Get effective auditor encryption key (asset-specific if set, otherwise global)
const auditorEK = await confidentialAsset.getAssetAuditorEncryptionKey({
  tokenAddress: TOKEN_ADDRESS,
});
```
